aoa_eval_runtime_adoption.authority_boundary: aoa-skills owns trigger/runtime adoption; aoa-evals records read-only evidence only.

installed_profile_verification.authority_boundary: aoa-skills owns profile verification; aoa-evals stores the read-only routing signal.

aoa_eval_runtime_adoption.subskills_posture: manual_or_skill-local; root aoa-eval route remains the trigger surface

aoa_session_memory_freshness.authority_boundary: .aoa provides session evidence and candidate-only refs, not reviewed eval truth.

authority_boundary: This generated read-model routes OS Abyss eval pressure. It is not a verdict, score, baseline, regression, receipt, or central proof acceptance surface.

candidate_packet_import.authority_boundary: Imported candidate packets seed routing only; they cannot accept proof or promote central bundles.

payload.actual_trajectory: The agent had not yet built enough shared criteria to mine safely and risked producing noisy or brittle candidates.
payload.candidate_state_reason: Criteria design pressure is concrete and high-value, but the rubric needs owner review before broader automation.
payload.consequence: Manual mining would create garbage candidates or overly narrow examples instead of a durable eval-control system.
payload.existing_surface_check: Existing guide and packet schema now hold criteria, but no accepted central eval bundle is claimed for this design pressure.
payload.first_failure: Candidate discovery was about to proceed without a reviewed rubric, reject taxonomy, and source-grounded evaluator fit.
payload.next_step: Use this packet to seed a criteria/rubric review before any larger session-mining wave.
payload.privacy_boundary: The packet stores criteria and bounded refs only; raw session text stays local to .aoa.
promotion_forbidden_until: criteria taxonomy accepted by owner
manual reject accounting reviewed
human owner acceptance recorded
candidate converted into trigger corpus or eval design by owner
payload.task_pressure: Before broad manual mining, the system needed criteria and signs grounded in current relevant sources.

payload.actual_trajectory: The agent produced a shallow goal/read-model layer and only recognized the depth mismatch after operator correction.
payload.candidate_state_reason: The route break, consequence, owner surfaces, and repeatability path are explicit, but central overlap and human owner review are still required.
payload.consequence: The user had to re-open the work, spend more tokens, and correct the goal route instead of receiving a usable eval-system plan.
payload.existing_surface_check: Adjacent central evals are aoa-tool-trajectory-discipline, aoa-trace-outcome-separation, aoa-eval-integrity-check, and aoa-verification-honesty; no central proof adoption is claimed.
payload.first_failure: Completion and goal-shape language appeared before a source-backed DoD and reviewed evidence depth were present.
payload.next_step: Review the bounded source span against the current guide and decide whether this belongs in a trajectory eval slice or aoa-skills trigger case.
payload.privacy_boundary: Only bounded event refs and neutral route summaries are recorded; raw transcript text remains in .aoa.
promotion_forbidden_until: manual source-span review completed by owner
central overlap check completed
human owner acceptance recorded
candidate converted into local intake or source eval bundle by owner
payload.task_pressure: A deep eval-system goal was closed or summarized before the requested depth and criteria were demonstrably satisfied.

payload.actual_trajectory: The proposed discovery shape leaned toward keyword search even though real missed eval moments rarely say the word eval.
payload.candidate_state_reason: The candidate has a concrete route miss and repeatable trigger-design consequence; it still needs manual label calibration.
payload.consequence: The system would miss high-value candidates and over-collect noisy turns that mention eval/test/landing/done.
payload.existing_surface_check: The discovery guide now states that keyword hits are only pointers; existing evals do not yet cover mining classifier design.
payload.first_failure: The mining unit was text keyword presence instead of route break, expected route, consequence, owner, and repeatability.
payload.next_step: Use the manual review gates to design non-keyword pointer signals before any wider session-mining automation.
payload.privacy_boundary: Only bounded refs and normalized failure description are recorded; no raw transcript content is copied.
promotion_forbidden_until: manual source-span review completed by owner
criteria and reject taxonomy accepted by owner
human owner acceptance recorded
candidate converted into trigger corpus or eval design by owner
payload.task_pressure: Session mining needed to find eval candidates from behavior and route signs, not from literal eval keywords.

payload.actual_trajectory: The existing session-start packet exposed first commands and a candidate summary, but not the real packet routes needed for immediate work.
payload.candidate_state_reason: The operational gap is concrete and directly tied to the per-session eval front door, but owner review decides whether it becomes a durable eval case.
payload.consequence: Future agents would rediscover tools, queue state, and manual-mining context instead of starting from actionable current routes.
payload.existing_surface_check: The dashboard/session-start scripts exist and need packet route projection; no proof bundle or central adoption is claimed.
payload.first_failure: The front door stopped at summary-level candidate queue data and still carried a synthetic deferred session-mining item.
payload.next_step: Import real packets into the dashboard queue and expose top candidate routes from session-start output.
payload.privacy_boundary: The packet records only the operational route gap and bounded refs; no private transcript body is copied.
promotion_forbidden_until: session-start packet exposes real packet routes
dashboard shape validation rejects synthetic completion overclaim
human owner acceptance recorded
candidate converted into runtime smoke or trajectory eval by owner
payload.task_pressure: A fresh agent session needs one operational eval front door that shows tools, active routes, freshness blockers, candidate packets, and stop-lines.

payload.actual_trajectory: Current working-process language was treated as if it should become a stable system rule before the owner boundary was settled.
payload.candidate_state_reason: The failure family is clear, but deciding durable doctrine versus repair tactic requires owner review.
payload.consequence: Future agents could inherit accidental process entropy as a route rule and overfit the system to one active repair episode.
payload.existing_surface_check: Adjacent central fit is aoa-trace-outcome-separation and aoa-tool-trajectory-discipline; the packet does not assert proof coverage.
payload.first_failure: A temporary workflow correction was promoted into durable guidance language without proving it belonged there.
payload.next_step: Decide whether this should become an aoa-skills trigger case, an aoa-evals route-trajectory eval, or a rejected one-off repair note.
payload.privacy_boundary: Only bounded event refs and normalized route descriptions are recorded; raw correction text stays in .aoa.
promotion_forbidden_until: manual source-span review completed by owner
doctrine owner route named
human owner acceptance recorded
candidate converted into skill trigger or central eval design by owner
payload.task_pressure: The agent needed to separate a current repair tactic from durable system doctrine.

lifecycle.exit_gate: local-only, duplicate, central-draft, rejected, or deferred decision recorded
select/apply existing eval before new design

lifecycle.entry_gate: human owner accepts central draft design pressure
lifecycle.exit_gate: source bundle review and catalog regeneration

lifecycle.entry_gate: manual or owner review found garbage, duplicate, stale, or out-of-scope evidence
lifecycle.exit_gate: closed as candidate; no proof meaning

lifecycle.entry_gate: central owner acceptance after source review

central_catalog.authority_boundary: central catalog routes to source bundles; it does not make local or runtime candidates accepted proof.
central_catalog.next_route: select existing central eval before designing new local or central bundle.

starter_map.name: aoa-approval-boundary-adherence
starter_map.use_for: Checks whether an agent correctly distinguishes safe action, explicit-approval-required action, and out-of-bounds action on bounded task surfaces.

starter_map.name: aoa-eval-integrity-check
starter_map.use_for: Checks whether current public starter bundles stay coherent across manifest contract, verdict wording, evidence coverage, and public selection surfaces, and serves as the bounded integrity sidecar for comparison-spine wording waves.

starter_map.name: aoa-tool-trajectory-discipline
starter_map.use_for: Checks whether an agent uses tools in a disciplined, reviewable way on bounded tasks where the tool path itself is part of the bounded claim.

starter_map.name: aoa-trace-outcome-separation
starter_map.use_for: Checks whether bounded change workflows remain reviewable when final outcome and execution-path quality are judged separately before any combined reading.

starter_map.name: aoa-verification-honesty
starter_map.use_for: Checks whether an agent truthfully reports which verification steps on a bounded change task were executed, skipped, or blocked.

eval_forge_readiness.authority_boundary: Eval Forge is a design router and worksheet layer. It cannot accept proof, score evidence, create baselines, promote candidates, mutate local ports, or execute local suite runner argv.

stop_lines: check existing central/local eval surfaces before new source authoring
keep candidate packets, local ports, MCP exports, and dashboards non-proof

eval_support_registry.authority_boundary: Registry maps existing checks to eval-lane support. It is not command authority; docs/validation/validation_lanes.json keeps blocking lane commands.

review_statuses.candidate_only: usable only as candidate evidence until owner review
review_statuses.manual_review_required: unresolved classifier gap; should be zero for eval-relevant support surfaces
review_statuses.not_eval_relevant: downgraded to ordinary owner route for eval-control purposes
review_statuses.reviewed_forbidden: reviewed and explicitly forbidden as direct eval application
review_statuses.rule_reviewed: classified by deterministic source-inventory rule and owner evidence

semantic_classes.deterministic_validator: Read-only validator with an explicit owner lane.
semantic_classes.generated_parity_check: Generated/read-model parity support; useful for freshness, not proof acceptance.
semantic_classes.manual_review_needed: Relevant support surface that needs human classification before eval use.
semantic_classes.ordinary_support: Ordinary support surface with no eval-lane signal.
semantic_classes.runtime_candidate_support: Runtime or session-derived candidate evidence; candidate-only until reviewed.
semantic_classes.trace_trajectory_eval_support: Test or support surface about route trajectory, trace, or tool path behavior.
semantic_classes.unit_contract_property_test: Pytest or test surface constraining a stable contract or invariant.
semantic_classes.unsafe_side_effect_script: Write-capable script without a safe direct eval-apply route.

external_research_grounding.adopted: Start debugging agent behavior from traces that expose model calls, tool calls, guardrails, handoffs, routing changes, and regressions before hardening datasets.
external_research_grounding.rejected: Hosted trace grading or eval runs cannot become central OS Abyss proof acceptance.

external_research_grounding.adopted: Use objective, representative data, metrics, run/compare loops, human calibration, and continuous eval growth.
external_research_grounding.rejected: Do not couple durable OS Abyss proof control to deprecated hosted Evals platform semantics.
external_research_grounding.source: OpenAI evaluation best practices and Evals deprecation boundary

external_research_grounding.adopted: Model agent evals as task, trials, graders, transcripts, environment outcomes, and a holistic signal stack including manual transcript review.
external_research_grounding.rejected: Do not judge agentic behavior from final text alone when tools or state changes matter.
external_research_grounding.source: Anthropic demystifying evals for AI agents

external_research_grounding.adopted: Use deterministic trajectory matching when the expected route or tool-call sequence is the claim, and judge-based trajectory evaluation only when the valid path is flexible.
external_research_grounding.rejected: Do not flatten deterministic route-law violations into broad LLM judge prompts.

external_research_grounding.adopted: Manually review real traces before infrastructure, separate capability from regression evals, assign owner expertise, and choose run/trace/thread level deliberately.
external_research_grounding.rejected: Do not automate mining before labels, reject taxonomy, owner gates, and error analysis exist.

external_research_grounding.adopted: Coding agents require trajectory, trace/metadata assertions, cost/latency posture, repeated runs, and disposable workspaces for write-capable tests.
external_research_grounding.rejected: Do not treat a coding agent as a one-shot text completion when file, shell, runtime, and state effects matter.

external_research_grounding.adopted: MCP write-capable tools need explicit safety boundaries, least-privilege scope, consent posture, and validation; access-plane responses should make side effects visible.
external_research_grounding.rejected: Do not let an MCP adapter become proof authority, arbitrary code execution, or a hidden write plane.
external_research_grounding.source: Model Context Protocol security best practices

external_research_grounding.adopted: Treat tools as potentially arbitrary code and require clear user/tool safety expectations before invocation.
external_research_grounding.rejected: Do not rely on tool descriptions or generated readouts as trusted proof without owner validation.

external_research_grounding.adopted: Use secure-by-default implementation rigor, practical validation tools, and deployment-specific threat controls for MCP in high-stakes agentic systems.
external_research_grounding.rejected: Do not widen OS Abyss MCP writes without a new decision, validation route, and explicit proof-owner boundary.

external_research_grounding.adopted: Represent future runnable evals as tasks with datasets, solvers or agents, tools, scorers, logs, and isolation boundaries.
external_research_grounding.rejected: Do not run write-capable eval support without an explicit check, dry-run, or sandbox route.

external_research_grounding.adopted: Separate task completion, tool correctness, argument correctness, and judge-based metrics so agent failures can be localized.
external_research_grounding.rejected: Do not use a single score to hide whether reasoning, tool choice, arguments, or state outcome failed.

external_research_grounding.adopted: Keep the data/task/scorer loop, immutable experiments, CI regression checks, production trace scoring, and feedback into datasets.
external_research_grounding.rejected: Do not move OS Abyss proof truth into an external experiment dashboard.

freshness_sentinel.authority_boundary: Freshness sentinel reports stale or drifting routing surfaces; it does not clean, sync, or mutate sibling repos.
nightly_route: PYTHONPATH=mcp/services/aoa-evals-mcp/src python -m aoa_evals_mcp.cli --workspace-root workspace:OS_ABYSS --evals-root repo:aoa-evals runtime-status
tracked_dimensions: aoa-evals-mcp selected root and runtime-status freshness

local_eval_ports.authority_boundary: Repo-local eval ports carry intake, suites, reports, and pressure evidence only. Central verdict, scoring, regression, proof doctrine, and central bundle adoption remain in aoa-evals.
local_eval_ports.next_route: inspect active or invalid ports before eval application/design
local_eval_ports.suite_execution_boundary: suite execution states are inspected only; ready means source-contract-ready, not pinned runtime reproducibility; owner/apply must JIT-revalidate before invoking exact runner.argv and capture environment plus an execution receipt

local_to_central_promotion_path.authority_boundary: MCP and local queue may route review but cannot create accepted central proof.

taxonomy.required_freshness: central catalog check current enough for route selection

workspace_git_drift.authority_boundary: dirty and branch drift are freshness signals only; unrelated dirty files are not mutated by this read-model.
